title: Constraint conservation Amazon gate-only follow-up

queries: confidence gated graph transfer negative transfer conservative prompt alignment
selective graph-to-token transfer reduce negative transfer domain-specific gating
graph representation transfer conservative residual injection confidence threshold

evidence.why_now: EXP198 narrowed Amazon-side transfer and improved user-ltv from worse to neutral, but user-churn still failed clearly while item-incoterms again preserved strong salt-side signal. The next justified follow-up is therefore to separate conservative gated transfer from the newer Amazon-side conservation losses themselves: keep the validated Part 1 base and strong salt-side path, but shrink Amazon-side post-alignment and identity regularization almost to zero before spending Optuna budget.

hypothesis: If the remaining Amazon regression after EXP198 comes less from gated residual mixing itself and more from the extra constraint-conservation penalties still coupled onto the Amazon tasks, then keeping a stricter confidence gate while nearly disabling Amazon-side post-alignment retention and entity-identity pressure should better isolate the useful conservative-transfer effect without sacrificing the strong salt-side continuation signal.
notes: This is a screening-only follow-up after EXP198 remained globally failed but improved user-ltv from worse to neutral while preserving strong item-incoterms continuation signal.

The intended mechanism split is narrow: keep confidence-gated conservative transfer on Amazon, but remove almost all added Amazon-side conservation penalties so the test isolates whether the residual transfer itself is still useful.
Do not run Optuna or final full test from this candidate directly.
The comparison remains fair because the validated Phase 2 per-task best hyperparameters stay fixed and only the Part 3 transfer/conservation controls are narrowed further.

Scientific interpretation still follows the Stage 3 task-aware rule set: user-ltv subset/full-test scale mismatch still applies, and item-incoterms should still be judged scientifically against the stored full-test reference band rather than only the optimistic subset baseline.
